title: salvia pratensis var. alba
intro: Salvia pratensis var. alba, also known as White Meadow Sage, is a flowering plant that belongs to the mint family and is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Salvia pratensis var. alba include reducing inflammation, improving cognitive function, and supporting the immune system, as well as exhibiting potential anticancer properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional remedies like salves and ointments.
